description:
You are a compliance officer who has received an offline snapshot of a server’s configuration.  
Your goal is to audit the snapshot for the system’s time-zone and locale settings and to write a
log file that can later be ingested by an automated checker.

Snapshot layout (already present on the system before you start):
1. Directory: /home/user/compliance_snapshot/etc  
   • /home/user/compliance_snapshot/etc/timezone  
     – Contains exactly one line with the system’s time-zone string.  
   • /home/user/compliance_snapshot/etc/locale.conf  
     – Follows the same syntax as a normal /etc/locale.conf file.  
       For this specific snapshot it contains two key-value lines.  
2. File: /home/user/compliance_snapshot/AUDIT_REFERENCE  
   • Contains a single ISO-8601 UTC timestamp that you must reuse verbatim in your report.

Required result of your work:
1. Create the directory /home/user/audit/logs/  (create parent directories if missing).
2. Inside that directory create the file /home/user/audit/logs/time_locale_audit.log
   with the following *exact* four-line format (no extra blank lines, no leading/trailing spaces):

   Line 1  Time Zone: <tz string from timezone file>  
   Line 2  Default LANG: <value of LANG= in locale.conf (without quotes)>  
   Line 3  LC_TIME: <value of LC_TIME= in locale.conf (without quotes)>  
   Line 4  Report Generated At: <the timestamp found in AUDIT_REFERENCE>

   Example (showing formatting only – your file must use real values from the snapshot):  
   Time Zone: America/New_York  
   Default LANG: en_US.UTF-8  
   LC_TIME: en_DK.UTF-8  
   Report Generated At: 2024-04-01T12:00:00Z

3. Do **not** hard-code any text except the fixed field labels shown above. All three data fields
   must be parsed from the snapshot files exactly as they are written there.

4. Once you are done, verify the contents of /home/user/audit/logs/time_locale_audit.log
   by printing it to the terminal.

The automated test will:
• Check that /home/user/audit/logs/time_locale_audit.log exists.  
• Verify that it contains exactly four non-empty lines.  
• Confirm that each line starts with the correct fixed label and that the values match what is
  stored in the snapshot.  
• Confirm that the file permissions allow reading (e.g. 0644 or more permissive).

Be sure to:
• Create any missing directories with appropriate user-writable permissions.  
• Read the snapshot files instead of querying the live system.  
• Preserve the exact case and spelling of all extracted values – do not trim or
  transform them.  
• Include the “Report Generated At:” line using the timestamp taken from
  /home/user/compliance_snapshot/AUDIT_REFERENCE.

When everything is correct, display the finished log file so the auditor can see the result.
truth:
Snapshot file contents that the automated checker will use:

1. /home/user/compliance_snapshot/etc/timezone  
   America/New_York

2. /home/user/compliance_snapshot/etc/locale.conf  
   LANG="en_US.UTF-8"  
   LC_TIME="en_DK.UTF-8"

3. /home/user/compliance_snapshot/AUDIT_REFERENCE  
   2024-04-01T12:00:00Z

Expected final audit log file:

Path: /home/user/audit/logs/time_locale_audit.log  
Content (exactly four lines, each terminated by \n):

Time Zone: America/New_York  
Default LANG: en_US.UTF-8  
LC_TIME: en_DK.UTF-8  
Report Generated At: 2024-04-01T12:00:00Z

The automated test will verify the file’s existence, readability, and exact byte-for-byte
contents as shown above.
